34: public abstract class RubyTestBase {
35: 
36:     public static final Path LS_HOME;
37: 
38:     static {
39:         Path root;
40:         if (System.getProperty("logstash.root.dir") == null) {
41:             // make sure we work from IDE as well as when run with Gradle
42:             root = Paths.get("").toAbsolutePath();
43:             if (root.endsWith("logstash-core")) root = root.getParent();
44:         } else {
45:             root = Paths.get(System.getProperty("logstash.root.dir")).toAbsolutePath();
46:         }
47: 
48:         if (!root.resolve("versions.yml").toFile().exists()) {
49:             throw new AssertionError("versions.yml not found is logstash root dir: " + root);
50:         }
51:         LS_HOME = root;
52: 
53:         initializeGlobalRuntime(LS_HOME);
54:     }
55: 
56:     static Ruby initializeGlobalRuntime(final Path currentDir) {
57:         String[] args = new String[] { "--disable-did_you_mean" };
58:         Ruby runtime = Ruby.newInstance(Logstash.initRubyConfig(LS_HOME, currentDir, args));
59:         if (runtime != RubyUtil.RUBY) throw new AssertionError("runtime already initialized. perhaps a Test class isn't subclassing RubyTestBase as it should?");
60:         return runtime;
61:     }

80:     @Test // @Tag("Performance Optimization")
81:     public void testRubyStringCloningMemoryOptimization() {
82:         ByteList bytes = ByteList.create("0123456789");
83:         RubyString original = RubyString.newString(RubyUtil.RUBY, bytes);
84: 
85:         RubyString result = Cloner.deep(original);
86:         assertNotSame(original, result);
87: 
88:         assertSame(bytes, original.getByteList());
89:         // NOTE: this is an implementation detail or the underlying sharing :
90:         assertSame(bytes, result.getByteList()); // bytes-list shared

92:         // but when string is modified it will stop using the same byte container
93:         result.concat(RubyUtil.RUBY.getCurrentContext(), RubyUtil.RUBY.newString(" "));
94:         assertNotSame(bytes, result.getByteList()); // byte-list copied on write
95:     }

69:     /**
70:      * Extract takes an arbitrary string of input data and returns an array of
71:      * tokenized entities, provided there were any available to extract.  This
72:      * makes for easy processing of datagrams using a pattern like:
73:      *
74:      * {@code tokenizer.extract(data).map { |entity| Decode(entity) }.each do}
75:      *
76:      * @param context ThreadContext

<source_slice path="logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/common/BufferedTokenizerExt.java" lines="80-114">
80:     @JRubyMethod
81:     @SuppressWarnings("rawtypes")
82:     public RubyArray extract(final ThreadContext context, IRubyObject data) {
83:         final RubyArray entities = data.convertToString().split(delimiter, -1);
84:         if (hasSizeLimit) {
85:             final int entitiesSize = ((RubyString) entities.first()).size();
86:             if (inputSize + entitiesSize > sizeLimit) {
87:                 throw new IllegalStateException("input buffer full");
88:             }
89:             this.inputSize = inputSize + entitiesSize;
90:         }
91:         input.append(entities.shift(context));
92:         if (entities.isEmpty()) {
93:             return RubyUtil.RUBY.newArray();
94:         }
95:         entities.unshift(input.join(context));
96:         input.clear();
97:         input.append(entities.pop(context));
98:         inputSize = ((RubyString) input.first()).size();
99:         return entities;
100:     }
101: 
102:     /**
103:      * Flush the contents of the input buffer, i.e. return the input buffer even though
104:      * a token has not yet been encountered
105:      *
106:      * @param context ThreadContext
107:      * @return Buffer contents
108:      */
109:     @JRubyMethod
110:     public IRubyObject flush(final ThreadContext context) {
111:         final IRubyObject buffer = input.join(context);
112:         input.clear();
113:         inputSize = 0;
114:         return buffer;
</source_slice>

96:         // Note: RubyHash.put calls JavaUtil.convertJavaToUsableRubyObject on keys and values
97:         map.forEach((key, value) -> hash.put(key, deep(runtime, value)));
98:         return hash;

<source_slice path="logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/RubyJavaIntegration.java" lines="200-214">
200: 
201:         /**
202:          * Support the Ruby union method on Java Collection.
203:          * @param context Thread context
204:          * @param self First Ruby collection object
205:          * @param other Second Ruby collection object
206:          * @return Ruby collection containing union of self and other
207:          */
208:         @JRubyMethod(name = "|")
209:         public static IRubyObject or(final ThreadContext context, final IRubyObject self,
210:             final IRubyObject other) {
211:             final Collection<?> dup = new LinkedHashSet<>(JavaUtil.unwrapIfJavaObject(self));
212:             dup.addAll(JavaUtil.unwrapIfJavaObject(other));
213:             return JavaUtil.convertJavaToUsableRubyObject(context.runtime, dup);
214:         }

45: public final class Rubyfier {
46: 
47:     private static final Rubyfier.Converter IDENTITY = (runtime, input) -> (IRubyObject) input;
48: 
49:     private static final Rubyfier.Converter FLOAT_CONVERTER =
50:         (runtime, input) -> runtime.newFloat(((Number) input).doubleValue());
51: 
52:     private static final Rubyfier.Converter LONG_CONVERTER =
53:         (runtime, input) -> runtime.newFixnum(((Number) input).longValue());
54: 
55:     private static final Rubyfier.Converter JAVAUTIL_CONVERTER =
56:             JavaUtil::convertJavaToRuby;
57: 
58:     private static final Map<Class<?>, Rubyfier.Converter> CONVERTER_MAP = initConverters();
59: 
60:     /**
61:      * Rubyfier.deep() is called by JrubyEventExtLibrary RubyEvent ruby_get_field,
62:      * ruby_remove, ruby_to_hash and ruby_to_hash_with_metadata.
63:      * When any value is added to the Event it should pass through Valuefier.convert.
64:      * Rubyfier.deep is the mechanism to pluck the Ruby value from a BiValue or convert a
65:      * ConvertedList and ConvertedMap back to RubyArray or RubyHash.
66:      * However, IRubyObjects and the RUby runtime do not belong in ConvertedMap or ConvertedList
67:      * so they are unconverted here.
68:      */
69:     private Rubyfier() {
70:     }
71: 
72:     public static IRubyObject deep(final Ruby runtime, final Object input) {
73:         if (input == null) {
74:             return runtime.getNil();
75:         }
76:         final Class<?> cls = input.getClass();
77:         final Rubyfier.Converter converter = CONVERTER_MAP.get(cls);
78:         if (converter != null) {
79:             return converter.convert(runtime, input);

101:     private static Map<Class<?>, Rubyfier.Converter> initConverters() {
102:         final Map<Class<?>, Rubyfier.Converter> converters =
103:             new ConcurrentHashMap<>(50, 0.2F, 1);
104:         converters.put(RubyString.class, IDENTITY);
105:         converters.put(RubyNil.class, IDENTITY);
106:         converters.put(RubySymbol.class, IDENTITY);
107:         converters.put(RubyBignum.class, IDENTITY);
108:         converters.put(RubyBigDecimal.class, IDENTITY);
109:         converters.put(RubyFloat.class, IDENTITY);
110:         converters.put(RubyFixnum.class, IDENTITY);
111:         converters.put(RubyBoolean.class, IDENTITY);
112:         converters.put(JrubyTimestampExtLibrary.RubyTimestamp.class, IDENTITY);
113:         converters.put(String.class, (runtime, input) -> runtime.newString((String) input));
114:         converters.put(Double.class, FLOAT_CONVERTER);
115:         converters.put(Float.class, FLOAT_CONVERTER);
116:         converters.put(Integer.class, LONG_CONVERTER);
117:         converters.put(
118:             BigInteger.class, (runtime, value) -> RubyBignum.newBignum(runtime, (BigInteger) value)
119:         );
120:         converters.put(
